english: without the skin, head, and entrails, če-śa (che sha) of a large -- čuń-śa (chung sha) of a small animal. -- śa-krág (sha khrag) flesh and blood, meton. 1. for body, śa-krág rtsál-ba (sha khrag rtsal ba) a sound body Mil. 2. for: children born of the same parents Cs. -- śa-rgyágs (sha rgyags) fat meat. -- *śa-čúg* (*śa bčug*) (sha bcug) meat cut into strips and hung up to dry in the sun W., C. (Hook. II, 183). -- śa-rjéń (sha rjen) raw meat. -- śa-nyóg (sha nyog) Sch.: 'soup with greens in it'. -- śa-rnyiń (sha rnying) old meat. -- śa-mdóg (sha mdog) colour of the skin, complexion Dzl. and elsewh. -- śa-mdog-lóg-pa (sha mdog log pa) Cs.: erysipelas, St. Anthony's fire? -- śa-nág (sha nag) the lean of meat Cs. -- śa-nád (sha nad) a certain disease Lt. -- *śá-na* (*sha na*) (lit.-sna) (sna) W. ardour, zeal? -- śá-spu (sha spu) feathers, downs. -- *śa-spíń* (*sha sping*) meat boiled down to jelly W. -- śa-próg (sha prog) Mil.? -- *śá-bhag-leb* (*sha bhag leb*) a sort of pie baked in oil C. -- śa-bo (sha bo) sheep, cattle or other animals destined for slaughter Mil. nt. -- śá-bu (sha bu) a maggot. -- *śa-búr* (*sha bur*) W. boil, abscess, ulcer; Sch.: mark left by a lash, weal. -- *śa-dé* (*sha de*) rice boiled with small pieces of meat C. -- śa-sbráń (sha sbrang) flesh-fly, blue-bottle-fly. -- śa-rméń (sha rmeng) fleshy excrescence, a little lump in the muscular flesh. -- śa - btsós (sha btsos) boiled meat. -- śa-tsá (sha tsha) 1. hot meat. 2. friend Pth., S.g., śa-tsa-ćan (sha tsha can) amicable, attached W. -- śa-tsuń dmár-po (sha tshun dmar po) Sch.: 'a tumour resembling a weal or a wart'. -- śa-tsil (sha tshil) the fat of flesh. -- śá-tsoń-pa (sha tshong pa) butcher, dealer in meat. -- śa-odzín (sha 'dzin) 1. a hook for taking meat out of a kettle C., W. 2. the fork of Europeans. -- śa-odzér (sha 'dzer) wart. -- śa-zá (sha za), śa-zán (sha zan) 1. prop.: flesh-eater, carnivorous animal. 2. gen.: a class of demons, described as fierce and malignant, Ssk. पिशाच. -- śa-zúg (sha zug), śa-yzúg (sha gzugs) = zug (zug) 2. -- śa-rág (sha rag) dried apricots, with little pulp, and almost as hard as stone. -- śa-ríd (sha rid) lean flesh. -- śa-ril (sha ril) 1. little meat-pies. 2. v. śa-ba (sha ba). -- śa-rúg (sha rug) sauce, gravy C. -- śa-rúl (sha rul) putrid meat. -- śa-ró (sha ro) a disease Wdn., is said to be an induration of the skin, callus, or perh. scirrhus. -- śa-rlóń (sha rlong) fresh meat, raw meat, śa-gsár (sha gsar) flesh of an animal that has just been killed.
1. Dzl. No, 1. Sch.: high water, flood, inundation. -- 2. Lt.: a certain hereditary disease or infirmity?
some kind of game (?) Wdn.
, ka-če śa-ka-ma (kha che sha ka ma) saffron C.
Cs. a kind of sugar.
, or śa-okón (sha 'khon), Wdn.; Sch.: grudge, resentment, hatred.

Ssk., pód-pa (phod pa) Tib., the mighty, the powerful, the bold, n. of the family of Buddha, the founder of the Buddhist religion, and hence often n. of Buddha himself, also śā-kya-túb-pa (shAkya thub pa) (Mil. rather boldly abbreviates it into śāk-túb (shAk thub)), śā-kya-mú-ni (shAkya mu ni), śā-kya-seń-ge (shAkya seng ge).
v. yśa-dkar (gsha dkar).
the cawing or croaking of ravens W.
Mńg.? perh. dough mixed with meat.

wylie: sha kha ma, sha kha ra
english: = śa-ka-ma (sha ka ma) and śa-ka-ra (sha ka ra).
Sch. a small bag or purse.
Sch. a shaggy dog, a poodle.
v. śa-kón (sha kon).
, col. for śam-gós (sham gos).
Lt.?
Lex., as explanation of dbal (dbal)?
, = klu (klu) Wdn.

1. also śa-dag (sha dag) mere, merely, only, kyeu śá-stag btsás-te (khye'u sha stag btsas te) only sons being born Dzl.; mi dbúl-pońs-pa śá-stag-ste (mi dbul phongs pa sha stag ste) as they are all of them poor people Dzl.; bdén-pa-mtoń-ba śá-stag-tu gyúr-to (bden pa mthong ba sha stag tu gyur to) they all come to the knowledge of the truth Tar. -- 2. Ld. for (rtai)śám(-la)-btags(-pa) ((rta'i) sham (-la) btags (-pa)) a pack, a bundle, fastened to the saddle behind the rider, *śá-stag-la kol (*sha stag la khol) or rel toń* (rel tong) tie it up, fasten it behind!
Ld., Pur. ape, monkey.
1. Ssk. शण hemp, Cs.: flax, śá-nai ras (sha na'i ras) Stg., Sch.: 'fine linen', śá-nai (sha na'i)
